mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is already above 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in >50% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains pinned near zero, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity market repricing and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of low-rate months, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year periods, or payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals corporate sector weakness that outweighs household demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding capacity utilization that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
